title: plantago maritima subsp. juncoides
intro: Plantago maritima subsp. juncoides, also known as sea plantain or buck's-beard, is a subspecies of the coastal plantain plant, which is a type of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The health benefits of Plantago maritima subsp. juncoides include its potential to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, which can be applied topically to wounds or ingested to support overall health.
